title: ADHS Program Review 2018 (Part 1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Update
Program: Alcohol Drug and Human Service Date: March 21, 2018
Faculty Completing Form: Jennifer Reynolds
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
The Alcohol Drug and Human Service (ADHS) program has made updates of courses to better fall in line
with California Consortium of Addiction Programs and Professionals (CCAPP) education accreditation.
This included changes in course units, student learning outcomes and delivery method. The college has
begun offering ADHS courses via video conference which has increased enrollment.
We are close to having an Associate Degree for Transfer (ADT) in Social Work and Human Services for
Transfer. Two courses need to be updated for this ADT, ADHS 1001 Introduction to Human Services and
ADHS 2020 Alcohol & Drug/Human Services Practicum. Course updates through eLumen will need to
take place and approved by the Curriculum Committee before these courses are ready to be approved
by California-ID Descriptors.
Updating and offering the Associate in Science Degree connected to the ADHS program will give
opportunity for students to earn a degree in this field. Also, having an associate degree provides an
incentive for student getting their Alcohol and Drug Credential. CCAPP identifies requirements for
applying for the Certified Alcohol Drug Counselor I (CADC-I) Credential without a degree requires 3,000
hours of supervised work experience while requirements for applying for the CADC-I Credential with an
Associate's Degree is 2,080 hours of supervised work experience. Roughly 1,000 less of supervise work
experience for students with an Associate’s Degree.
An AHDA Handbook will be put together to identify policy and procedure for student in the ADHS
program.
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
Program Learning Outcomes:
1. Describe current practices in human services and addiction prevention, treatment, and recovery as
they relate to criminal justice, human development and health relations within the culture as a whole.
2. Analyze, compare, and contrast the themes of human service needs and their significance within the
American culture.
3. Analyze and describe physiological and pharmacological aspects of substance abuse and dependence.
4. Demonstrate how drugs affect different body systems, cause side effects, and produce different
addictive and abusive behaviors within the population.
5. Apply knowledge, skill, and understanding for competent performance in Alcohol and Drug and
Human Services settings.

-- 1 of 2 --

title: ADHS Program Review 2018 (Part 2)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Update
The PLOs are being assessed on the basis of CCAPP education accreditation. Courses have been and are
being updated to provide best practice and knowledge for this specific certificate per CCAPP.
There is no assessment tool put into place at this time.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
An obstacles identified is one regarding policy and procedures of the ADHS program. Specifically, issues
in student relapse/recovery. To combat such issues, an ADHS Handbook will be developed to identify
issues concerning program disqualification, mental and emotional readiness into the practicum, code of
ethic and code of conduct for alcohol/drug counselors and human service providers.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications. (Attach completed CQIP form.)
There are no specific program budgetary needs at this time. Ideas for future needs, student drug
testing, guest speaker(s), workshops, conferences, trainings for ADHS instructor(s).
